message:
fix: depth_limit=0 in DFS should return no edges

When depth_limit=0 is passed to dfs_edges, the expectation is that only
the source node is "visited" but no edges are traversed. Previously,
neighbors at depth 1 were being pushed onto the stack before the depth
check, causing [(source, neighbor)] to be returned.

The fix adds an explicit `max_depth > 0` check before pushing the
initial set of neighbors/successors onto the stack.

Fixes dfs_edges, dfs_edges_directed (Rust core) and the Python
dfs_forest_undirected/dfs_forest_directed helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
